title: perideridia parishii subsp. parishii
intro: Perideridia parishii subsp. parishii, also known as Parish's yampah or desert yampah, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States. It belongs to the carrot family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Perideridia parishii subsp. parishii include infusions, teas, and salves that can be used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential side effects.
